prompt: Drop in the call that safely binds the value so the query is injection-proof.
explanation: setString binds `name` as a bound parameter, so user input can never change the query's structure — the fix for SQL injection (CWE-89). The other method names don't exist on PreparedStatement.

prompt: Drop in the function that makes the username safe to place inside HTML.
explanation: html.escape turns `< > &` into HTML entities, so injected markup is shown as text, not executed — the fix for reflected XSS (CWE-79). `quote` is for URLs; `str`/`repr` don't neutralise HTML.

prompt: Drop in the argument so the hostname can't inject extra shell commands.
explanation: An argument list with shell=False means `host` is a single argument the shell never parses, so `; rm -rf /` can't run (CWE-78). Building a command string invites shell injection.

prompt: Drop in the expression so a user can't escape the uploads folder with `../`.
explanation: os.path.basename strips any directory parts (including `../`), keeping the file inside UPLOADS — the fix for path traversal (CWE-22). The raw filename lets `../../etc/passwd` escape.

prompt: Drop in the secure way to hash a password for storage.
explanation: bcrypt is a slow, salted password hash built to resist brute force (CWE-916/327). MD5 and SHA-1 are fast and unsalted — wrong for passwords; Base64 is encoding, not hashing.

prompt: Drop in the value so the API key isn't hardcoded in the source.
explanation: Reading the secret from an environment variable keeps it out of source control (CWE-798). Hardcoding a literal key or password exposes it to anyone who can see the code.

prompt: Drop in the safe way to parse data coming from an untrusted client.
explanation: json.loads only produces plain data structures — it can't execute code. pickle.loads, yaml.load and eval can all run attacker-controlled code on untrusted input (CWE-502).

prompt: Drop in the cookie attribute value that helps mitigate CSRF.
explanation: SameSite=Lax stops the browser sending the session cookie on cross-site requests, blocking most CSRF (CWE-352). `None` allows cross-site sending; `Disabled`/`All` aren't valid values.
